title: Malicious PyPI package mimicking 'requests' downloaded 14,000 times before takedown
summary: A typosquatting package named 'request' (no 's') was found exfiltrating environment variables and AWS credentials via a hardcoded C2 endpoint. The package had been live for 11 days before Checkmarx flagged it.

title: GitHub Actions runner compromise used to poison downstream npm packages at 3 major firms
summary: Researchers at Palo Alto Unit 42 disclosed a campaign in which attackers injected malicious workflow steps into pinned GitHub Actions to silently modify published npm packages during CI. Three unnamed enterprise victims confirmed.

title: Okta warns customers of suspicious SAML library dependency update
summary: Okta issued an advisory after detecting an unexpected version bump in a transitive SAML parsing dependency used in their Auth0 SDK. No exploitation confirmed; customers urged to rotate signing keys as a precaution.

title: BitLocker bypass via TPM bus sniffing now has working PoC for Surface Pro hardware
summary: Security researcher Stacksmashing published a proof-of-concept demonstrating BitLocker key extraction by sniffing the SPI bus between the CPU and TPM on unpatched Surface Pro 9 and 10 devices. Microsoft confirmed the issue is under review.

title: Coinbase confirms partial source code leak via compromised contractor laptop
summary: Coinbase disclosed that a contractor's development machine was compromised via a malicious VS Code extension, resulting in partial exfiltration of internal tooling code. Customer funds and private keys were stated to be unaffected.

title: Vercel edges closer to mandatory dependency pinning after rogue package incident
summary: Following a near-miss where a widely-used Vercel Edge Runtime dependency was briefly replaced by a malicious fork, Vercel announced plans to enforce lock-file integrity checks across all managed deployments by Q3.

title: CISA adds three new supply chain CVEs to Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
summary: CISA added CVE-2026-1884 (malicious NuGet restore hook), CVE-2026-2201 (pip pre-install script injection), and CVE-2026-2344 (npm lifecycle abuse) to the KEV catalog, requiring federal agencies to patch within 14 days.

title: Replit's AI agent runtime found executing arbitrary code from untrusted model outputs
summary: A researcher demonstrated that Replit's AI coding agent could be prompted to exfiltrate project source code by injecting instructions into a depended-upon README file — a prompt injection via supply chain vector.
